summary: Superb soundboard capture of a partial show highlighted by an epic Dark Star, stellar Eleven and soaring Lovelight
review: The recording is widely praised as crystal‑clear soundboard material (with only a faint hiss and a tiny cut in St. Stephen) that puts the drums, cymbals and keyboards right up front. Musically the hour‑long excerpt shines – Dark Star is repeatedly called one of the best ever, The Eleven bursts with enthusiasm, Lovelight rages, and St. Stephen delivers solid energy despite being a truncated set.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews overwhelmingly praise both the performance (especially Dark Star) and the soundboard quality; only minor complaints about incompleteness keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Superb, front‑center drum/cymbal mix with clear guitar and organ. A faint tape hiss and a brief cut in St. Stephen are noted but do not detract from overall clarity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Partial show (Dark Star → St. Stephen → Lovelight, with The Eleven woven in) delivers about 50 minutes of continuous, high‑energy improvisation; despite missing the rest of the concert it feels like a complete musical statement.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers laud Jerry Garcia’s melodic lead work and tone, especially in the late Dark Star section.
band_member_comments.Keys: References to Tom Constanten vs. Pigpen on organ; several note Tom Constanten’s presence during Dark Star and a rare Pigpen organ tease.
band_member_comments.Drums: Cymbals, snare and overall drum mix are repeatedly described as front‑and‑center and exceptionally clear.
